questions.question: A bacterial colony starts with 100 cells and doubles every hour. How many cells will there be after 3 hours?
questions.explanation: After 1 hour: 100 × 2 = 200. After 2 hours: 200 × 2 = 400. After 3 hours: 400 × 2 = 800. Or use the formula: 100 × 2³ = 100 × 8 = 800.

questions.question: A car depreciates at a rate of 10% per year. If it costs $20,000 today, what will it be worth after 1 year?
questions.explanation: After 1 year, the car retains 90% of its value: $20,000 × 0.9 = $18,000.

questions.question: An investment grows according to the formula A = 500(1.05)^t, where t is the time in years. What is the initial investment?
questions.explanation: In the exponential growth formula A = P(1 + r)^t, the coefficient P represents the initial amount. Here, P = 500.

questions.question: A radioactive substance has a half-life of 4 days. If you start with 160 grams, how much will remain after 8 days?
questions.explanation: After 4 days (one half-life): 160 ÷ 2 = 80 grams. After 8 days (two half-lives): 80 ÷ 2 = 40 grams.

questions.question: Write an exponential decay model for a quantity that starts at 1000 and decreases by 20% each year.
questions.explanation: Decay by 20% means the quantity retains 80% each year. The model is A = initial amount × (decay factor)^t = 1000(0.8)^t.

questions.question: If a population of 50 rabbits triples every 2 years, how many rabbits will there be after 4 years?
questions.explanation: After 2 years: 50 × 3 = 150. After 4 years (two periods of 2 years): 150 × 3 = 450.
